description_long: This object is the core of an apple with both ends of the fruit still attached, showing a partially eaten state. The skin is mostly red with some yellow-green patches, and the flesh is exposed around the core, revealing seeds and fibrous textures. The stem is still present on one end, while the other end shows the calyx. The surface of the apple skin is slightly glossy and smooth, while the exposed flesh appears moist and slightly rough. The core is cylindrical, with visible seed pockets and a fibrous, off-white interior. The overall shape is irregular due to the eaten portions, and the object appears lightweight and organic.
description: A partially eaten apple core with red skin, exposed seeds, and a visible stem.
description_view_0: Shows the red skin, stem, and one end of the apple core.
description_view_1: Displays the calyx end and exposed core with visible seeds.
description_view_2: Highlights the cylindrical core and seed pockets, with both ends visible.
description_view_3: Focuses on the red skin and stem, with less of the core exposed.
